topics.title: AI Infrastructure
topics.executive_summary: Alpha Compute went live with a 504-GPU cluster in Canada and signed a $32.2M, two-year AI deal; Digi Power X committed $35M to NVIDIA's next-gen Vera Rubin systems for Q1 2027 deployment.

stories.headline: Alpha Compute Launches First GPU Cluster with 504 NVIDIA B200s
stories.body: <cite index="8-1,8-5">Alpha Compute deployed its first GPU cluster in Canada with 504 NVIDIA B200 GPUs after closing a $32.2 million, two-year off-take agreement with a leading frontier AI research laboratory</cite>, projecting annualized revenue run-rate of approximately $23 million.

stories.headline: Digi Power X Commits $35M to NVIDIA's Next-Gen Vera Rubin Platform
stories.body: <cite index="5-2,5-3">Digi Power X committed $35 million to acquire NVIDIA Vera Rubin systems—NVIDIA's rack-scale successor to Blackwell—with initial deployment targeted for Q1 2027</cite> to expand its GPU-as-a-Service operations.
